author: Opher
msg:
Just looked at rewards and I see some interesting reactions to my reconfiguration from 1-2 days ago. 
I had 6 Boxes on 2 ISP’s. Originally getting the full rewards, until recently. So now I have an ISP for every box. 4 boxes are in location 1 and 2 boxes in location 2. The 2 boxes in location 2 received .16 - .24 with the 2 NEW ISP’s. The 4 boxes in location 1 received mixed results. For example, 2 boxes with 2 new ISP’s got .16 - .24 . But 2 boxes that were on the older existing ISP’s only got .01 - .03 . Kind of weird how the already existing ISP’s that previously had 3 boxes each attached was still receiving .01 - .03 with only one box attached to it now, when my other 4 exact setups with NEW ISP’s are getting .16 - .24. I went ahead and factory reset those 2 older( older as in already existing before the new ones) gateways, reset the 2 boxes and set them back up to the gateways. So maybe that’ll refresh those two systems..

author: Dave
msg: Because before you reach the end service you are sent through another IP

author: Dave
msg:
Blocking VPNs works through identifying the VPN servers and blocking these.

If you use a residential proxy you are not sent through a VPN server but a residential proxy. Basically through another household.

author: Kolks
msg: Eventually EVERYTHING will be taken into account. They need only time. It takes 2 lines of code to filter out IP addresses with regards to used phones/licenses (the x > too many scenario) . The only way to be "save" in the long run is to use SIM cards. Maybe this can work with 1 or 2 SIM cards per box (or more depending on the rewards you want). Let's see (measure) where the sweet spot is. Maybe there will be a decent reward for farmboxes  connected to the Internet with some other tasks in the future. The task(s) right now are not. That's for sure. And I don't blame them for that. It was fun while it lasted. At least for me and some of us.
